reviewContent:
## ExpressVPN Review 2026: The Premium Choice Gets More Accessible

ExpressVPN has long been considered the gold standard for VPN performance and security, but its premium pricing kept many users at bay. In 2026, ExpressVPN has shaken things up with new tiered pricing plans that bring the entry price down to $2.44/month, alongside continued speed innovations with Lightway Turbo. We tested every aspect of the service to determine whether ExpressVPN still justifies its premium reputation.

### Our Verdict

ExpressVPN earns a 4.6/5 rating in 2026. It remains one of the fastest VPNs available, delivers rock-solid security through its TrustedServer RAM-only infrastructure, and operates from the privacy-friendly British Virgin Islands. The new tiered pricing structure makes it significantly more affordable than before, though it still costs more than budget options like Surfshark. The Kape Technologies ownership remains a point of contention, but ExpressVPN's independently audited no-logs policy and BVI jurisdiction provide strong counterarguments.

### Speed Test Results

ExpressVPN continues to set the pace for VPN speed performance in 2026. Using the Lightway UDP protocol, we measured just a 5-6% speed drop on local servers -- essentially imperceptible for everyday use. One test recorded 89.17 Mbps on a 100 Mbps baseline, demonstrating the minimal overhead of the Lightway protocol.

Long-distance connections performed admirably, with ExpressVPN consistently retaining 75-85% of base speed even on transatlantic and transpacific routes. This translates to fast enough throughput for 4K streaming, large file downloads, and competitive online gaming without noticeable lag.

The standout speed development for 2026 is Lightway Turbo, which uses multi-lane tunneling to send more data simultaneously through the VPN tunnel. ExpressVPN's internal testing claims up to 330% speed improvements with Lightway Turbo enabled. While real-world results are more modest, the performance gains are noticeable, particularly on high-bandwidth connections. Lightway Turbo is currently available on the Windows v12 app when using Lightway UDP or Automatic protocol selection, with rollout to other platforms expected throughout 2026.

### The Lightway Protocol

Lightway is ExpressVPN's proprietary VPN protocol, and it's a genuine engineering achievement. With approximately 2,000 lines of code -- far fewer than OpenVPN or WireGuard -- the protocol is lean, fast, and easy to audit. It's fully open source, with the codebase available on GitHub for public scrutiny.

Lightway connects near-instantly (typically under a second), supports built-in obfuscation for use in censored regions, and handles network transitions seamlessly. Switching between Wi-Fi and mobile data doesn't drop your VPN connection, which is a significant quality-of-life improvement for mobile users.

The protocol uses ChaCha20 encryption by default, with AES-256 also supported. Both are considered military-grade encryption standards. The small codebase means a smaller attack surface, reducing the potential for undiscovered vulnerabilities.

### Security and Privacy

ExpressVPN's security architecture is built around TrustedServer technology, which was genuinely pioneering when introduced and has since been adopted (in various forms) across the industry. Every ExpressVPN server runs entirely on volatile RAM -- no hard drives, no persistent storage. When a server is powered down or rebooted, all data is completely wiped. This means even if a server were physically seized, there would be no stored data to recover.

In June 2025, KPMG completed an independent audit verifying both ExpressVPN's no-logs policy and the proper functioning of TrustedServer technology. The audit confirmed that the servers operate as described and that no user activity logs are retained.

ExpressVPN is incorporated in the British Virgin Islands, a jurisdiction with no data retention laws. Critically, foreign governments (including the US and UK) cannot directly compel a BVI company to produce customer records -- they must petition the BVI High Court, which operates under BVI law. This provides a meaningful legal barrier that jurisdictions like the US, EU, or Five Eyes nations cannot easily circumvent.

The kill switch (called Network Lock) is available on all major platforms and functions reliably, blocking all internet traffic if the VPN connection drops unexpectedly. DNS leak protection and IPv6 leak protection are enabled by default.

### Kape Technologies Ownership

ExpressVPN was acquired by Kape Technologies in September 2021 for $936 million. Kape also owns Private Internet Access, CyberGhost, and ZenMate, making it one of the largest VPN conglomerates in the world. In May 2023, Kape was taken private by Unikmind Holdings Limited, owned by Israeli-Cypriot businessman Teddy Sagi.

This ownership structure raises legitimate questions. Kape Technologies (formerly Crossrider) had a controversial past in the adware business before rebranding and pivoting to cybersecurity. However, ExpressVPN maintains that it operates independently, with its own development team and infrastructure. The independently verified no-logs policy and BVI jurisdiction provide tangible safeguards regardless of corporate ownership.

Users must decide for themselves whether this corporate structure is a dealbreaker. For most, the technical and legal protections in place are sufficient. For those with heightened threat models, providers with no corporate parent (like Mullvad) may be preferable.

### Streaming and Unblocking

ExpressVPN remains one of the most reliable VPNs for streaming in 2026. It consistently unblocks Netflix (multiple libraries), Disney+, BBC iPlayer, Amazon Prime Video, Hulu, and virtually every other major streaming platform we tested.

The MediaStreamer feature (Smart DNS) allows streaming on devices that don't support VPN apps, such as gaming consoles and smart TVs. Setup requires a simple DNS configuration change and works without encrypting all traffic, which means faster streaming speeds at the cost of reduced privacy on those specific devices.

Server switching is rarely needed -- most ExpressVPN servers work with major streaming platforms on the first try, which is a testament to the company's investment in keeping its IP addresses unblocked.

### Apps and User Experience

ExpressVPN offers apps for Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, Android, Fire TV, and routers. Browser extensions are available for Chrome, Firefox, and Edge. The app design is clean, minimalist, and focused on a single large connect button with easy access to server locations.

The router app is a particular strength. ExpressVPN's custom router firmware (Aircove) provides whole-home VPN protection and allows different devices to connect to different server locations simultaneously. This is ideal for households where family members need to access content from different regions.

With the new tiered plans, device limits vary: the Basic plan supports 10 simultaneous connections, Advanced supports 12, and Pro supports 14. This is a significant improvement from the previous flat limit of 8 devices and brings ExpressVPN more in line with competitor offerings.

### Pricing Breakdown

ExpressVPN's 2026 pricing restructure introduces three tiers:

- **Basic (2-Year Plan):** $2.44/month (81% discount, includes 4 free months) -- VPN only, 10 devices
- **Advanced (2-Year Plan):** $3.49/month -- Adds Keys password manager, Advanced Protection (malware/tracker blocking), Identity Monitoring with fraud alerts
- **Pro (2-Year Plan):** $5.24/month -- Adds dedicated IP address, higher eSIM data allowance, 14 simultaneous devices
- **1-Year Plan:** From $3.49/month for Basic
- **Monthly Plan:** $12.95/month

All plans include a 30-day money-back guarantee. There is no free trial, but the money-back guarantee effectively serves as a risk-free testing period.

The new pricing makes ExpressVPN dramatically more competitive. At $2.44/month for the Basic 2-year plan, it's within striking distance of budget providers while offering premium infrastructure and the Lightway protocol advantage.

### Server Network

ExpressVPN operates over 3,000 RAM-only servers across 105 countries and 170+ locations. While the raw server count is lower than competitors like Surfshark (4,500+) or NordVPN (6,000+), every server runs on the TrustedServer architecture, which is a meaningful quality advantage. The network covers all major regions comprehensively, with strong representation in North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific.

For most locations, the physical server and registered IP address are in the same country. A small number of virtual server locations have the IP address matching the chosen country while the physical hardware is located elsewhere -- these are clearly labeled in the app.

### Who Should Use ExpressVPN?

ExpressVPN is the ideal VPN for:

- **Speed-focused users** who want the fastest possible VPN connection for streaming, gaming, or large downloads
- **Privacy-conscious users** who value BVI jurisdiction and RAM-only server architecture
- **Streamers** who need reliable, first-try unblocking of international content
- **Mobile users** who benefit from Lightway's instant connections and seamless network switching
- **Router users** who want whole-home VPN protection with the Aircove firmware
- **Users in censored regions** who need built-in obfuscation to bypass VPN blocks

It may not be the best choice for extremely budget-conscious users (Surfshark offers more for less), those uncomfortable with Kape Technologies ownership, or users who need unlimited simultaneous connections.

### FAQ

**Is ExpressVPN safe to use?**
Yes. ExpressVPN uses TrustedServer RAM-only architecture, has been audited by KPMG (June 2025) confirming its no-logs policy, and is based in the privacy-friendly British Virgin Islands. The Lightway protocol is open source and has been independently audited.

**Is ExpressVPN still owned by Kape Technologies?**
Yes. Kape Technologies acquired ExpressVPN in September 2021 for $936 million. Kape was subsequently taken private by Unikmind Holdings Limited in 2023. ExpressVPN states it operates independently with its own team and infrastructure.

**How many devices can I connect simultaneously?**
The Basic plan supports 10 devices, Advanced supports 12, and Pro supports 14 simultaneous connections. This is an increase from the previous limit of 8.

**What is Lightway Turbo?**
Lightway Turbo is an enhancement to ExpressVPN's proprietary Lightway protocol that uses multi-lane tunneling to increase speeds. Internal tests showed up to 330% improvement. It's currently available on Windows with broader platform support coming in 2026.

**Does ExpressVPN work with Netflix?**
Yes. ExpressVPN reliably unblocks multiple Netflix libraries and virtually all major streaming platforms including Disney+, BBC iPlayer, Amazon Prime Video, and Hulu.

**Is ExpressVPN worth the price?**
With the new 2-year Basic plan at $2.44/month, ExpressVPN is now competitively priced. If you prioritize speed, security, and privacy jurisdiction, it offers strong value. The Advanced and Pro plans add useful extras like a password manager and dedicated IP.
